title: Create a Multi-Container Pod
description:
## Multi-Container Pods

Pods can contain multiple containers that share the same network namespace and storage volumes. This is often used for the 'sidecar' pattern.

**Your task:**

Create a Pod named `web-sidecar` in the `default` namespace with two containers:
1. Name: `app`, Image: `nginx:alpine`
2. Name: `sidecar`, Image: `busybox:1.36`, Command: `sleep 3600`

Verify it is running:
```bash
kubectl get pod web-sidecar
```

hints.title: Manifest Structure
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: web-sidecar
spec:
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
  - name: sidecar
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
EOF

commands.command: kubectl get pod web-sidecar -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].name}' | wc -w | tr -d ' '
